User.title: User
User.description: Represents a customer account in the JamaStore application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for account creation and notifications.

telegramUsername.description: The user's Telegram username, linked for receiving automated order status notifications.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Admin.title: Admin
Admin.description: Represents an administrator user with permissions to manage the store's content and orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Admin entity.

email.description: The admin's email address for authentication and contact.

name.description: The full name of the administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the admin account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the admin account was last updated.

Look.title: Look
Look.description: Represents a curated outfit or lookbook entry, often consisting of multiple products, displayed with multi-language support.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Look entity.

titleEn.description: The title of the look in English.

titleUz.description: The title of the look in Uzbek.

titleRu.description: The title of the look in Russian.

descriptionEn.description: A detailed description of the look in English, potentially AI-generated for SEO.

descriptionUz.description: A detailed description of the look in Uzbek, potentially AI-generated for SEO.

descriptionRu.description: A detailed description of the look in Russian, potentially AI-generated for SEO.

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the high-resolution main image of the look.

thumbnailImageUrl.description: URL to a lower-resolution thumbnail image for previews.

adminId.description: Reference to the Admin who created or last updated this look. (Relationship: Admin 1:N Look)

productIds.description: References to Products that are part of this look. (Relationship: Look N:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the look was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the look was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual clothing item available in the store's catalog, with multi-language descriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

nameEn.description: The name of the product in English.

nameUz.description: The name of the product in Uzbek.

nameRu.description: The name of the product in Russian.

descriptionEn.description: A detailed, SEO-friendly description of the product in English, potentially AI-generated.

descriptionUz.description: A detailed, SEO-friendly description of the product in Uzbek, potentially AI-generated.

descriptionRu.description: A detailed, SEO-friendly description of the product in Russian, potentially AI-generated.

price.description: The current price of the product.

currency.description: The currency in which the product is priced (e.g., 'UZS', 'USD').

availableSizes.description: A list of available sizes for this product (e.g., 'S', 'M', 'L', 'XL', 'XXL').

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the high-resolution main image of the product.

thumbnailImageUrl.description: URL to a lower-resolution thumbnail image for previews.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

UserMeasurement.title: UserMeasurement
UserMeasurement.description: Stores a user's physical measurements and preferences for the AI Smart Size Advisor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserMeasurement entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User associated with these measurements. (Relationship: User 1:N UserMeasurement)

gender.description: The user's gender, used for size recommendations (e.g., 'male', 'female', 'other').

desiredFit.description: The user's preference for clothing fit (e.g., 'tight', 'regular', 'loose').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when these measurements were recorded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when these measurements were last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase order, with status management and notification capabilities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'New', 'Confirmed', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including shipping and taxes.

currency.description: The currency of the order's total amount (e.g., 'UZS', 'USD').

shippingAddress.description: The full shipping address for the order.

billingAddress.description: The full billing address for the order, if different from shipping.

telegramNotificationSent.description: Flag indicating if an automated Telegram notification for this order's status has been sent to the customer.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by an administrator regarding the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product item within an order, detailing the specific product, quantity, and price at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent Order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

priceAtOrder.description: The unit price of the product at the time the order was placed.

size.description: The specific size of the product item ordered (e.g., 'S', 'M', 'L').

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. Access is restricted to the owner of the user profile, identified by `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores administrative user profiles. The existence of a document in this collection for a given UID signifies an admin role, used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC).

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the administrator, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Publicly accessible curated outfits (lookbook entries). Read access is public for all users, while write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: lookId
params.description: The unique ID of the look.

definition.description: Publicly accessible catalog of clothing items. Read access is public for all users, while write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the product.

definition.description: Stores personal physical measurements and preferences for the AI Smart Size Advisor, private to each user. Includes denormalized `userId` field (matching the path parameter) for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the owner user.

params.name: measurementId
params.description: The unique ID of the user's measurement record.

definition.description: User-specific purchase orders. Users can manage their own orders, and administrators have full access. Includes denormalized `userId` field (matching the path parameter) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Individual product items within a specific order. Access is inherited from the parent order, ensuring that only the order owner or an administrator can view or modify order items. Includes denormalized `userId` and `orderId` (derived from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific item within the order.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for JamaStore is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs (Query-Agnostic Rules).

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This principle is achieved by eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which inherently breaks atomic operations and adds complexity. Instead, authorization-relevant fields are denormalized:
*   For **User-specific private data** (e.g., `/users/{userId}/measurements/{measurementId}` and `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`), the `userId` is present both in the document path and denormalized as a field within the document itself (e.g., `UserMeasurement.userId`, `Order.userId`). This enables security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == userId` directly, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic without needing to read parent documents.
*   For **OrderItems** (`/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/items/{orderItemId}`), the `userId` and `orderId` from the hierarchical path directly provide the necessary authorization context. This inherently ties access to the specific user's order without additional lookups.
*   For **Publicly accessible, Admin-managed data** like `/looks` and `/products`, authorization for write operations relies on a global role check. This check (`exists(/admins/$(request.auth.uid))`) is entirely independent of the specific document's content, further simplifying rules and ensuring independence.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports efficient and secure `list` operations, meaning security rules are not used to filter results, but to validate access to *all* documents a query might return.
*   **Public Collections:** `/looks` and `/products` are designed for public read access. Any user can efficiently list and browse these collections as there are no inherent security filters required for reads.
*   **Private User Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/measurements` and `/users/{userId}/orders` are structured as subcollections. This path-based ownership ensures that a user can only query and list their *own* measurements and orders, as `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This inherently restricts `list` operations to the authenticated user's data, making rules simple (`allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **Admin Access:** Administrators (identified via the `/admins/{adminId}` collection for DBAC) have broad read/write access. While a single query for *all* orders across all users might require advanced indexing or Cloud Functions (not covered by basic security rules), the current structure maintains strong security for user-specific listing and allows admins to access specific orders by path.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
*   Collections like `/looks` and `/products` hold data with a consistent security posture (public read, admin write). 
*   User-specific data (`measurements`, `orders`) is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring all documents within these subcollections share the same ownership-based security requirements.
*   The `/admins` collection is dedicated solely to storing admin roles, separating it from general user profiles or application data.

**Access Modeling & DBAC:**
*   **Private Data:** `/users/{userId}`, `/users/{userId}/measurements`, `/users/{userId}/orders` consistently use a path-based ownership model, directly tying access to `request.auth.uid == userId`.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The `/admins/{adminId}` collection serves as the central point for Database-Backed Access Control. An authenticated user is granted 'admin' privileges if a document exists at `/admins/$(request.auth.uid)`. This allows for simple, performant existence checks in security rules to grant elevated privileges.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
*   Standardized authorization fields, such as `userId` and `orderId`, are used consistently across relevant collections.
*   Wildcard names (`{userId}`, `{lookId}`, `{orderId}`) are descriptive and semantically clear.
*   The `Order` entity explicitly uses a `status` field for clear state management, rather than relying on inferred states from multiple booleans or timestamps. This makes the application logic and security rules more straightforward.

This robust structure provides a clear, secure, and maintainable foundation for the JamaStore application, minimizing the complexity of Firestore Security Rules and directly supporting all critical application features outlined.
